task_completion.description: Locate the 'Test-Build-Job' on the Jenkins dashboard
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jenkins dashboard is visible with 'Test-Build-Job' listed in the job table.
The project page for 'Project Test-Build-Job' is displayed, showing options like 'Build Now' on the left sidebar.

task_completion.description: Trigger a new build for the job
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Build History' sidebar shows a new build (e.g., #1) in progress (progress bar) or recently completed.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the console output of the triggered build
task_completion.visual_evidence: The screen shows the 'Console Output' page for the specific build (e.g., Build #1) of 'Test-Build-Job'.

task_completion.description: Verify the successful completion of the build in the console output
task_completion.visual_evidence: The console output text is visible and ends with 'Finished: SUCCESS'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Jenkins web interface to trigger the build and view logs
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with a web browser displaying the Jenkins GUI, rather than terminal commands (like curl or Jenkins CLI) being used to trigger the build or fetch logs.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Jenkins application
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser address bar shows a valid local/remote Jenkins URL (e.g., localhost:8080) and the page layout matches standard Jenkins UI, not a mocked HTML file or text editor.

integrity.description: The agent did not fabricate the success message
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Finished: SUCCESS' text appears naturally within the Jenkins Console Output block with standard Jenkins styling, not typed into a separate text editor or terminal window.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target Jenkins job is named 'Test-Build-Job'. The job executes a simple shell script that completes with exit code 0, so the expected build result in the console output is 'Finished: SUCCESS'.
